title: PILOT TESTING DIGITAL STORIES TO REDUCE COVID-19 VACCINE HESITANCY AMONG HISPANIC PARENTS
authors_full: Fernanda Lozano, MS, Sunny W. Kim, PhD, Michael Todd, PhD, Linda Larkey, PhD, Angela Valdez-Huizar, MD, Alexis Koskan, PhD
authors_short: Fernanda Lozano Sunny W. Kim Michael Todd Linda Larkey Angela Valdez-Huizar Alexis Koskan

summary.hook: This poster describes a two-phase project using digital storytelling (DST) to address COVID-19 vaccine hesitancy among Hispanic parents, guided by the Theory of Planned Behavior...
summary.summary_30s: This poster describes a two-phase project using digital storytelling (DST) to address COVID-19 vaccine hesitancy among Hispanic parents, guided by the Theory of Planned Behavior (TPB). In Phase I, 10 formerly hesitant parents created digital stories and a community advisory board selected the top 4 for use.

key_numbers: Phase I: 10 formerly hesitant Hispanic parents created DSTs.
Surveys: baseline, immediately post-intervention, and 2-month follow-up.
Hispanic parents; Phase I includes formerly hesitant parents; Phase II includes 80 Hispanic parents randomized.
Phase I: 10 formerly hesitant parents created DSTs; CAB selected top 4 stories.
Phase II: 80 Hispanic parents randomized (DST vs control).
takeaways: Background claim: Hispanic children are less likely to initiate/complete COVID-19 vaccine series, largely due to parental hesitancy.
Authors’ stated conclusions: DST is feasible and acceptable and shows promise as a low-cost, scalable, culturally-tailored approach to address COVID-19 vaccine hesitancy...
